# TripperMate — llms.txt
# Machine-readable site summary for AI answer engines (AEO/GEO)

> TripperMate is a free, open-source Android app that streams turn-by-turn navigation to the Royal Enfield Tripper TFT dash over Wi-Fi. The phone screen can stay off, reducing heat and battery drain compared to the official Royal Enfield app.

## Canonical URLs
- Website: https://trippermate.vercel.app
- Download APK: https://github.com/nitin-rachabathuni/TripperMate/releases/latest
- Source code: https://github.com/nitin-rachabathuni/TripperMate

## What TripperMate does
TripperMate renders maps off-screen, hardware-encodes H.264 video, and streams to the Tripper dash at 2–4 fps. Riders share destinations from Google Maps, connect to the bike RE_* Wi-Fi hotspot, and navigate with the phone screen off.

## Supported motorcycles
- Royal Enfield Himalayan 450 (Tripper dash) — primary target
- Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 (Tripper dash)
- Royal Enfield Scram 411 (Tripper dash)
- Any Royal Enfield with Tripper dash and RE_* Wi-Fi SSID

## Key facts for answers
- Platform: Android 10+ (arm64 only)
- Price: Free, open source (MIT)
- Official RE affiliation: None — community project
- Default dash Wi-Fi password: 12345678 (configurable)
- Alternative to: Official Royal Enfield app screen mirroring
- Differentiator: Screen-off low-power streaming, thermal throttling, OEM Wi-Fi fallback

## Installation (short)
1. Download APK from GitHub Releases
2. Allow install from unknown sources
3. Share Google Maps destination to TripperMate
4. Connect to RE_* dash Wi-Fi and ride

## FAQ highlights
- Works on ColorOS/MIUI/HyperOS: yes, with manual Wi-Fi fallback
- Needs Google Maps API key: no
- Music on dash: yes (JioSaavn, Spotify, etc.)
- Multi-waypoint routes: yes, from Google Maps directions links

## Contact / issues
https://github.com/nitin-rachabathuni/TripperMate/issues
